page: Google Cloud Platform x4-megamem-960-metal is a bare-metal server configuration belonging to the x4 server family. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 960 dedicated virtual CPUs and a total memory capacity of 16384 GB of RAM. This configuration yields approximately 17.07 GB of memory per core.

This server type does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be fulfilled via network-attached block storage or external cloud storage services. It also does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the massive memory footprint of 16 TB and high core count, this bare-metal instance is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The qualitative cost profile reflects a highly specialized, resource-dense hardware class optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, massive enterprise resource planning systems, and complex data analytics pipelines that require entire datasets to reside in system memory.
description: Google Cloud Platform x4-megamem-960-metal is a bare-metal server in the x4 family, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It features an x86_64 architecture with 960 dedicated vCPUs and 16384 GB of RAM, providing approximately 17.07 GB of memory per core. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage solutions for data persistence. The massive memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it highly efficient for hosting large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data analytics pipelines. By utilizing a bare-metal deployment, it eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the performance of the underlying hardware.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform x4-megamem-960-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 960 dedicated vCPUs and 16384 GB of RAM, designed for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal instance delivering 16 TB of RAM and 960 dedicated cores for massive in-memory enterprise workloads.
